entities.name: Ilya
entities.context: Created the triangle model for pricing structure

entities.name: Triangle model
entities.context: Three-axis pricing framework using job descriptions, seats, and time

entities.name: Job descriptions
entities.context: One axis of pricing model—number of JDs processable within contract

entities.name: Seats
entities.context: One axis of pricing model—number of users (recruiters/hiring managers) with access

entities.name: Time
entities.context: One axis of pricing model—length of agreement (6 vs 12 months)

entities.name: Customers
entities.context: Prospective buyers who express uncertainty about JD volume and seat usage

entities.name: Recruiters
entities.context: Type of user occupying a seat in the pricing model

entities.name: Hiring managers
entities.context: Type of user occupying a seat in the pricing model

entities.name: Contract duration
entities.context: Proposed as natural bounding mechanism for pricing packaging

relationships.context: Ilya created and uses the triangle model
JD processing capacity is one axis of the model
User seats is one axis of the model
Agreement length is one axis of the model
Recruiters occupy seats in the pricing model
Hiring managers occupy seats in the pricing model
Customers cannot precisely forecast JD volume
Customers want to keep seat numbers under control
Customers prefer shorter initial trial periods (e.g., 6 months)
Duration serves as the bounding mechanism for pricing
